brand.name: Con la tuya, contribuyente
brand.tagline: Every peso the Uruguayan state spends, traceable.

money.scaleHelp: The gold bar under each amount is logarithmic and uses the same scale across the site: you can compare at a glance without reading the digits.
money.primaryNote: Amounts are converted to Uruguayan pesos when the source reports a foreign currency. The original currency is shown.
money.mixedCurrency: This contract has amounts in more than one currency.
money.convertedFrom: Amount converted to pesos from {currency}, at the exchange rate on the award date.

common.searchPlaceholder: Search by subject, supplier, or agency
common.name: Name

home.title: Follow the money.
home.lead: Every tender, award, and supplier of the Uruguayan state since {yearFrom}. Official open data, searchable.
home.ledgerHelp: What the state awarded most recently, according to the published data.
home.trendsHelp: Total awarded per year, in Uruguayan pesos.
home.anomaliesHelp: Contracts that depart from the usual pattern. A flag is not an accusation: it's a starting point for review.
home.sourceNote: Source: Compras Estatales open data (OCDS), catalogodatos.gub.uy.

contracts.title: Contract explorer
contracts.lead: Filter {total} contracts by agency, supplier, amount, year, and procedure.
contracts.resultsHelp: Totals computed over the applied filters.
contracts.histogramHelp: Number of contracts per year within the applied filters.

empty.title: No contracts match these filters
empty.body: Remove a filter or widen the year range.

emptyInitial.title: Search or filter to start
emptyInitial.body: Type what the state bought, or pick an agency in the filter panel.

contracts.tooMany: Showing the first {shown}. Narrow the filters to see fewer results.
contracts.needFilter: Add a filter (year, agency, supplier or amount) to see totals.

filters.title: Filters
filters.searchHelp: Searches the tender subject, the items, the agency, and the supplier.
filters.statusHelp: Only 1 in 12 records reports a status. Filtering by status leaves the rest out.
filters.methodHelp: The source reports no procedure on 7 of every 10 records.
filters.hasAmountHelp: Some official records publish no amount.
filters.tagHelp: Each stage of the process is its own record. Only the award reports a supplier and an amount.

notFound.title: No contract with that identifier
notFound.body: The identifier may have changed at the source. Try searching for it in the explorer.

contract.officialSourceHelp: Opens the official record for this contract on comprasestatales.gub.uy.
contract.itemsHelp: Awarded items with quantity and unit price.
contract.noItems: The official record lists no items for this contract.
contract.noDocuments: No documents are published for this contract.
contract.rawHelp: The OCDS document as the state publishes it.

stageHelp.award: The state awarded the purchase to a supplier. This is the stage that reports amounts.
stageHelp.awardUpdate: A correction to an already published award.
stageHelp.tender: Published call. No supplier or amount yet.
stageHelp.tenderUpdate: A clarification or answer to questions about a tender.
stageHelp.tenderAmendment: A change to the tender conditions.

contract.noMoneyStage: This stage reports no amounts. The figure appears once the award is published.
contract.itemsTenderHelp: Items included in the tender. Prices appear only once it is awarded.

suppliers.title: State suppliers
suppliers.lead: {total} companies and individuals that sold to the Uruguayan state.

empty.title: No supplier matches that search
empty.body: Try part of the name, without the legal form: “saceem” rather than “SACEEM S.A.”.

detail.byBuyerHelp: Agencies ranked by amount awarded to this supplier.
detail.concentrationHelp: How much of their revenue comes from their largest buyer.
detail.dataNote: The source publishes no breakdown by year, agency or category for this supplier — only the total and its contracts.

notFound.title: No supplier with that identifier
notFound.body: Try searching for it in the directory.

buyers.title: Buying agencies
buyers.lead: {total} Uruguayan state agencies that run procurement.

empty.title: No agency matches that search
empty.body: Try part of the name: "salud" instead of "Dirección General de la Salud".

detail.yearsHelp: Every year this agency recorded purchases. Tap a year to see those contracts.
detail.recordHelp: Amounts by year, supplier and category come from the public contract record, which only reports figures from {year} onwards. The total above comes from the agency summary and covers every year.
detail.bySupplierHelp: Suppliers ranked by amount received from this agency.
detail.bySupplierNote: Each contract’s amount is credited in full to every awarded supplier: the source does not split it between them, so on multi-supplier contracts these figures are an upper bound.
detail.categoriesHelp: Categories with the most recorded spending, by the classification on each awarded item.
detail.concentrationHelp: How much of their spending goes to their largest supplier.
detail.contractsHelp: This agency’s largest contracts in the public record.
detail.noAmounts: The public record reports no amounts for this agency.

notFound.title: No agency with that identifier
notFound.body: Try searching for it in the list.

anomalies.title: Price flags
anomalies.lead: Contracts where the unit price departs sharply from what the state usually pays for the same item. A flag is not an accusation: it marks something worth a look.
anomalies.disclaimer: There are legitimate explanations: urgent purchases, different specifications under the same catalogue code, or items misclassified at the source.

method.title: How it is calculated
method.body: For each combination of item, currency and unit of measure, a price baseline is built from the last 36 months. Each award's unit price is then scored against that baseline using a robust measure in log space: it uses the median rather than the mean, so a huge contract cannot mask its own anomaly.
method.scope: At least 10 comparable items are needed to score a price, and 30 to assign the highest severities.
method.rangeNote: The "usual range" runs from the 25th to the 95th percentile of that baseline, in the contract's original currency.

severityHelp.critical: More than 10 robust deviations above the baseline.

typeHelp.price_spike: The unit price departs from what is usually paid for that item.

empty.title: No flags for these filters
empty.body: Try another severity or clear the year filter.

generic.title: This data didn't load
generic.body: The query failed. Retry; if it keeps failing, it's on us.

timeout.title: The query took too long
timeout.body: Add a year or agency filter to narrow the search.

notFound.title: This page doesn't exist
notFound.body: The link may be broken, or the content moved.

rateLimit.title: Too many queries
rateLimit.body: Wait a minute and try again.

home.title: Con la tuya, contribuyente — Uruguay's public procurement
home.description: Explore {count} Uruguayan state contracts: who buys, who sells, and how much is paid. Official open data, searchable and comparable.

contracts.title: Uruguayan state contract explorer
contracts.description: Search and filter Uruguay's public contracts by agency, supplier, amount, year, and procedure type.

contractDetail.title: {title} — {buyer}
contractDetail.description: {buyer} awarded {amount} to {supplier} on {date}. Full contract detail, items, and official documents.

suppliers.title: Suppliers to the Uruguayan state
suppliers.description: Directory of {total} suppliers to the Uruguayan state, with amounts received, buying agencies, and contracts.

supplierDetail.title: {name} — state supplier
supplierDetail.description: {name} received {amount} from the Uruguayan state across {contracts} contracts with {buyers} agencies.

buyers.title: Buying agencies of the Uruguayan state
buyers.description: Directory of Uruguay's public agencies, with total spending, suppliers, and contracts.

anomalies.title: Flags in Uruguay's public procurement
anomalies.description: Uruguayan state contracts that depart from the usual pattern of price, supplier, or frequency.

about.title: How it works
about.lead: Where this data comes from and what you can do with it.
about.sourceBody: Everything you see here comes from the open data the Uruguayan state publishes in OCDS format (Open Contracting Data Standard), through catalogodatos.gub.uy and the Compras Estatales site. We don't add or edit records: we import them, normalize them, and make them searchable.
about.scaleBody: Every amount carries a logarithmic bar beneath it. It uses the same scale across the site, so you can compare one contract to another without reading the digits. Gold is reserved for money: if it isn't public money, it isn't gold.
about.totalTitle: Why we don't show a total
about.totalBody: A handful of official records carry corrupt quantities, and that breaks any sum. The clearest case: a UTE award for generator equipment rental declares a quantity of 1,200,007 units at USD 519,788.85 each. That comes to USD 623 billion — about eight times the country's GDP — for a single contract.
about.totalBody2: Three records like that hold 86% of all reported spending; thirteen hold 93%. A "total awarded" headline would, in practice, be the sum of those errors. So we show the median instead: half of the state's purchases are smaller than that figure. It doesn't break when one record does.
about.totalBody3: We don't delete them. Filter to that agency and you'll see the record exactly as the state publishes it, with a link to the official file. The strange number is part of the story.
about.limitsBody: Some official records report no amount, and amounts in foreign currency are shown with their original currency. A contract carrying a flag isn't necessarily irregular: it's a contract that departs from the pattern and is worth a look.
about.linkBody: Every contract links to its official record on comprasestatales.gub.uy. If something here doesn't add up, the source wins.

footer.disclaimer: Independent site. Not an official site of the Uruguayan state.
